content:
[S1]: I wasn't always the scary monster people say I am; once, I was just a lonely creature looking for a friend.  
[READER]: Hmm, a scary monster who just wants a friend? That sounds interesting! I want to know more about why they are lonely.  

[S2]: Every night, I would watch the children playing from the dark forest, wishing someone would notice me.  
[READER]: Aww, that’s kind of sad. Why don’t they come closer? I hope the monster isn’t mean.  

[S3]: But when I tried to say hello, my big voice scared them away, and I felt even more alone.  
[READER]: Oh no! That sounds really sad. I wonder if the monster can find a way to be less scary.  

[S4]: One night, a small child wandered near my home, and I wanted to keep them safe from the cold and dark.  
[READER]: That’s nice! So maybe the monster is actually good? I want to know what happens to the child.  

[S5]: I gently wrapped my big, warm arms around them, hoping to give comfort, but they screamed and ran.  
[READER]: Oh no, the child was scared! But I feel bad for the monster too. It’s like a big hug gone wrong.  

[S6]: I didn’t mean to frighten anyone; all I ever wanted was a friend to share the night with.  
[READER]: That makes me feel sorry for the monster. I hope someone can understand it someday.  

[S7]: Maybe one day, someone brave will see past my scary face and hear the kindness inside.  
[READER]: Yeah! I hope that happens because the monster sounds lonely but nice.  

[S8]: Until then, I’ll keep watching and waiting, hoping for a friend who isn’t scared of me.  
[READER]: That’s a little sad but also hopeful. I want to see the monster find a friend soon!
